mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive SPX returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5 percent annually, or the mechanism shows negative IC in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of months following CPIAUCSL MoM increases above 0.4 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across multiple rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the predictive power reverses sign in tightening regimes.
